summary: Fiery Half-Step opener, soaring Truckin’, and a smoking Wharf Rat
blurb: The show bursts open with a blistering Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo and tight folk‑rock numbers like New Minglewood Blues and Tennessee Jed. Highlights include a monster Truckin’ finish, a scorching Wharf Rat jam, and strong performances from Jerry and the rhythm section. The setlist balances high‑energy peaks with a few uneven moments.
review:
On November 6, 1977 at Broome County Arena, the Dead delivered one of the late‑'70s’s most exciting nights. An electrifying Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo kicked off the first set, setting a high bar that was sustained through Jack Straw, Tennessee Jed and a crisp New Minglewood Blues. While Mexicali Blues and Passenger sagged a bit, the band rebounded with tight renditions of Friend Of The Devil, Dire Wolf and The Music Never Stopped.

The second set built momentum despite a sluggish Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain intro, exploding into a spectacular sequence: Good Lovin’, Saint Stephen → Drums → Not Fade Away → Wharf Rat → Saint Stephen → Truckin’. That chain produced one of the era’s most memorable Wharf Rat jams and a thunder‑ous Truckin’ that sent the crowd home on an exuberant Johnny B. Goode encore. Across all recordings, Jerry’s “wolf‑guitar” tone roared atop the mix, Phil laid down a frantic yet solid bass foundation, Bob’s vocal grit shone on New Minglewood Blues and Tennessee Jed, Keith supplied sparkling piano flourishes early on, and Bill (with Mickey’s complementary textures) kept the drums tight, especially during the St. Stephen > Drums transition.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four analyses are marked positive, repeatedly praising the same standout moments – the Half‑Step opener, Truckin’ finish, Wharf Rat jam and strong Jerry leads – while noting only minor weak spots. Consistent high praise across soundboard, matrix and audience sources yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller soundboard capture with excellent mix, no dropouts and full instrument separation – the definitive listening experience.

key_highlights: Blazing Mississippi Half-Step opener sets an instant high.
Monster Truckin’ finish that closes the second set on fire.
Smoking Wharf Rat jam within the Saint Stephen > Drums > Not Fade Away sequence.
Tight New Minglewood Blues and Sunrise showcase strong vocal interplay.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s wolf‑guitar tone roars throughout, delivering soaring leads on Half‑Step, Truckin’ and a fiery solo that sits atop the mix.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a frantic sand‑worm energy and solid melodic foundation, anchoring the jams especially during extended improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals shine on New Minglewood Blues and Tennessee Jed; his interplay with Jerry adds a crafty, devilish edge to the set.
band_performance.Keith: Keith supplies sparkling piano sweeps early in the show (notably on Sunrise) but slips into a slightly sloppy percussive feel later, especially during the second‑set slowdown.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart adds texture and fills that complement Bill’s steady drumming; his contributions are most felt in the St. Stephen > Drums transition.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann keeps a tight, consistent groove throughout, driving the energy on Truckin’ and providing solid back‑beat during the Wharf Rat jam.
